info.title: Landlord Auth API
info.description: The Landlord authentication service. A multi-tenanted usage of AWS Cognito.

post.summary: Impersonate a role within a tenant.
Impersonate a user within a tenant.
Clear any impersonation for the current user.
Set a new password after being issued a temporary password.

TenantSetDescriptionRequest.description: Set the description for a tenant

TenantRolesRequest.description: Set the roles for a tenant

NoContent.description: The request completed successfully.  No response body will be sent.

LoginResponse.description: A response for a successful authentication

ObtainTokenResponse.description: A response to obtain tokens via the OAuth2 PKCE flow

AssociateOTPResponse.description: Return the secret for associating an OTP device.

TenantsResponse.description: Return the tenants available to the user.

TenantRolesResponse.description: Return the roles associated with the tenant

TenantUsersResponse.description: Return the users associated with the tenant

ImpersonateResponse.description: Return a new token for the impersonated role.

RecoverPasswordResponse.description: A response for a successful initiation of a password recovery

SetNewPasswordResponse.description: A response for a successful setting of a new password

BadRequest.description: An error occurred.  The client provided one or more invalid inputs.

Unauthorized.description: An error occurred.  The client is not authenticated in the system.

errors.description: The client should authenticate itself to get the requested response.

Forbidden.description: An error occurred.  The client is not authorized to perform the requested action.

errors.description: The client does not have the access right for the content.

NotFound.description: An error occurred.  The client requested a resource that does not exist.

errors.description: Could not find to requested resource.

InternalServerError.description: An unexpected error occurred during the request.

errors.description: An error occurred while processing the request.

oauth2.description: This API uses OAuth 2 with the implicit grant flow. [More info](https://api.example.com/docs/auth)

destination.description: Displayable string for the destination where the recovery code was sent.
